title: The Fifth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: June 23, 2024

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Fifth Sunday after Pentecost falls in Ordinary Time, the longest liturgical season following Pentecost and preceding Advent. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, this date would feature readings typically from 1 Samuel (likely the David and Goliath account or similar) in the Old Testament, Psalm 9 or similar, 2 Corinthians (possibly 5:1-10 on the resurrection body), and Mark's Gospel (likely 4:35-41 on Jesus calming the storm, or 5:21-43). This Sunday emphasizes themes of faith, trust, and God's power amid uncertainty.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of its 80th General Convention held in Louisville, Kentucky (June 19-27, 2024), occurring during this exact sermon week. Major convention votes on LGBTQ+ inclusion, marriage theology, and climate action were being finalized. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kimberly Chastain (elected 2022), was engaged with ongoing national church restructuring and pastoral discussions around the convention's outcomes.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early summer heat in mid-June with temperatures in the mid-80s Fahrenheit. Colorado Republican Party internal divisions were intensifying ahead of the 2024 general election, with debates over candidate direction. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where Saint John's Cathedral is located, was in the midst of ongoing discussions about affordable housing and neighborhood development pressures. No major weather emergencies or local disasters marked this specific week.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Joe Biden's viability as a 2024 candidate was under intense scrutiny following a poor debate performance on June 27 (just days after this sermon), though the full crisis emerged in the following week. The Supreme Court had recently issued major decisions on affirmative action (late June) affecting higher education nationwide. The presidential campaign between Biden and presumptive Republican nominee Donald Trump was intensifying as summer progressed. Abortion rights remained a dominant political issue in the post-Dobbs decision landscape.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine continued as a grinding conflict in its third year, with active fighting ongoing in eastern Ukraine. The Gaza conflict following October 7, 2023, remained a source of global tension with ongoing humanitarian concerns and diplomatic efforts. The Paris Olympics were being prepared for opening in early August 2024, with international attention focused on global sport and French politics.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The American cultural moment in late June 2024 was characterized by political anxiety and election-year divisiveness, with deep uncertainty about the direction of national leadership. The Denver Nuggets' NBA championship run from the previous season (2023) had energized local sports culture, though the 2024 season was in offseason. LGBTQ+ pride celebrations had just concluded nationwide (June is Pride Month), creating both celebratory and contentious cultural conversations that directly intersected with the Episcopal Church's General Convention deliberations occurring that same week.
